pundit.name: Eric Wynalda
pundit.take: **"Gayden might as well have drafted with a blindfold on, but even that wouldn't explain his obsession with mid-table mediocrity."** You've got England taking on Ghana, and if Gayden's draft strategy was any indication, I expect him to backpass his way into yet another disastrous showing. While England is certainly a team of pedigree, as a former World Cup player let me remind you that pedigree doesn’t guarantee performance, just like Gayden's ill-fated choice of Jordan. "The Backpass Assassin" seems to have forgotten that Japan's precision pays dividends only in cartoons, not in the World Cup. Meanwhile, Zach "Mustard Boy" continues with his FIFA-like strategy of picking big names without understanding team chemistry. His reliance on Brazil to carry him is like wearing golden boots in a mud fight. This clash at Foxborough? England will roll the dice and scrape a win, proving once again that Gayden knows just enough to confuse himself into thinking he's a manager.

pundit_takes.headline: Gayden's England set to backpass their way past Ghana
pundit_takes.subtitle: Rumor has it Gayden plans to redefine playing it safe.

pundit_takes.headline: Bubba G's DR Congo poised to take a nap vs Colombia
pundit_takes.subtitle: Gunner's strategic relaxation might cost him his top spot.

pundit_takes.headline: Mustard Boy's Austria quietly exits World Cup unnoticed by fans
pundit_takes.subtitle: Zach's team clocks in zero points and zero fanfare.

pundit_takes.headline: Rafe's Germany still searching for victory in father's shadow
pundit_takes.subtitle: The Noisemaker could use a little more noise from his team.

rome_analytics_quotes: Zach sits fourth, but Brazil's still waiting to samba; 0 points today!
Rafe's draft is a comedy special; my grandma could've picked better, son.
Rafe spanks Devin with a +6 differential; dad must be beaming with pride!
Gunner's odds are slipping like he just saw a teenager named Rafe.
